unit.note: F0 fresh gate green. Client vitest 204 files / 1980 passed + npm run build green; server vitest 166 files / 1924 passed + 1 skipped (1925) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + npm run build green; tsc --noEmit + oxlint clean on the touched files. New D15 coverage lives in the NEW client/src/hooks/useTaskTranscript.test.ts (9 vitest fake-timer tests): F21 terminal-state stop (done + launch_failed each fetch exactly once then freeze; active keeps polling), the external-review cross-taskId-switch guard (a prior done task's state must not leak into a newly-selected running task even when its first tick rejects), F22 single-source modelName (the hook exposes the last `"model":"..."` from content; null when absent), and the pure extractModelName helper (last-wins / no-model / empty). Counts: 1980 client + 1924 server = 3904 passed / 3905 total (the +1 is the 1 long-standing skipped server test).

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes (Step 3.8) of the two boundaries this iterate touches, run REAL via vitest. (1) Transcript-content -> extractModelName parse boundary (producer = Claude's JSONL `"model":"..."` events; consumer = the header's model label): probed multi-model (last wins -> claude-opus-4-8), no-model (-> null), and empty content (-> null); a prior in-header regex had no null/empty guard path exercised -> the pure helper now pins all three. (2) Poll-lifecycle state-machine boundary (producer = server-reported task.state on each tick; consumer = the tick's terminal-state stop): the initial fake-timer probe reproduced F21 (done task polled 6x across 5 s instead of 1x); fixed via the latestStateRef; a follow-up probe from external code review found a SECOND bug (the ref outlived the effect, so a done->running taskId switch with a failing first tick inherited the stale `done` and stopped) -> fixed via a per-effect-run ref reset -> re-probe clean. Two consecutive clean probe rounds after the last fix => asymptote reached, both boundaries calibrated. Edge not probed: a real multi-second wall-clock browser session (the fake-timer model is deterministic and faithful to the sequential-poll setTimeout cadence; a live browser adds no discriminating signal for poll-count/stop logic).

e2e.note: No Playwright run. This iterate changes client transcript-poll LOGIC only (client/src/hooks/useTaskTranscript.ts + its new test, plus a prop-plumbing change in TaskDetailHeader.tsx / TaskDetailPage.tsx). The observable UI (transcript pane, header model label, live updates) is behavior-preserving; the fix (poller stops on terminal state; one poller per page) is a time+state-dependent lifecycle assertion best pinned by deterministic fake-timer vitest, which an e2e cannot exercise more faithfully. No Vite-config / proxy / WS-origin / CORS / route surface changed. The real TaskDetailHeader (TaskDetailHeader.test.tsx + .phone.test.tsx) and the real TaskDetailPage (TaskDetailPage.test.tsx) render green under RTL with the new prop wiring, covering the render path. F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification).

surface_verification.justification: This iterate is a behavior-preserving fix to the CLIENT transcript poller (useTaskTranscript) that consumes FR-01.12 (stateless byte-range transcript read). detect_frontend_changes reports has_frontend_changes=true (client/** touched), but there is no NEW browser-observable behavior to smoke: the transcript still renders, the header still shows the model label, and live updates behave identically - the only changes are (1) the poller stops when the task is done/launch_failed as its docblock already promised, and (2) the header no longer runs a duplicate second poller (its model label now arrives as a prop from the page's single poller). Both are poll-count / lifecycle assertions that a browser smoke (which confirms 'page loads with no console errors') cannot discriminate; they are pinned deterministically by the 9 fake-timer vitest tests in useTaskTranscript.test.ts (AC2 RED-first on pre-fix main: the done-task stop test fetched 6x instead of 1x; green after) and the render path is covered by the real TaskDetailHeader + TaskDetailPage RTL tests (48 passed). No Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS/route change. F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - the transcript poller stops on a terminal state (done / launch_failed) and each open task page runs exactly ONE poller: useTaskTranscript tracks the freshest server-reported state in a ref (reset per taskId) so the terminal-state stop fires; TaskDetailHeader's own useTaskTranscript is removed and the model label flows from TaskDetailPage's single poller as a prop. The F21/F22 scenarios no longer reproduce.
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.test.ts asserts a done (and a launch_failed) task fetches exactly once then freezes across a 5 s fake-timer advance, while an active task keeps polling (>=4 fetches), and that a done->running taskId switch keeps polling even when the new task's first tick rejects. modelName is exposed by the single poller (last `"model":"..."` wins; null when absent). TaskDetailHeader.test.tsx + .phone.test.tsx + TaskDetailPage.test.tsx stay green with the prop wiring.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression test is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after.
behaviors.evidence: NEW client/src/hooks/useTaskTranscript.test.ts. RED captured on pre-fix code: the F21 done-stop test asserted 1 fetch but the stale-closure poller fired 6 (across the 5 s advance) -> 'expected 1, got 6'; the F22 modelName tests + extractModelName unit tests failed with 'extractModelName is not a function' / undefined modelName. GREEN after the fix: 9/9 passed. The external-review cross-taskId-switch test is RED without the per-effect ref reset (only 1 fetch after the switch) and GREEN with it.

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full client (1980) + server (1924/+1 skipped) suites + both builds stay green; no invariant/ADR regressed.
behaviors.evidence: Client vitest 204 files / 1980 passed + npm run build green; server vitest 166 files / 1924 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + npm run build green; tsc --noEmit clean. Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact - useTaskTranscript still only GETs /transcript and writes nothing into ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json.

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted.
behaviors.evidence: Footprint: client/src/hooks/useTaskTranscript.ts (126 -> 162), client/src/hooks/useTaskTranscript.test.ts (new, 191), client/src/components/external/TaskDetailHeader.tsx (230 -> 220, shrank), client/src/pages/TaskDetailPage.tsx (676 -> 676, net-zero: one JSX prop added). TaskDetailPage.tsx stays exactly at its baseline current=676 (not ratcheted); TaskDetailHeader.tsx + useTaskTranscript.ts are not baselined and are < 300. TaskDetailPage.test.tsx (baseline current=362) was NOT modified - all new tests live in the un-baselined useTaskTranscript.test.ts. Serial #15, deps/blocks none, D01-D14 merged -> no live parallel collision.
